search_queries_used: site:diabetesjournals.org 2026 pharmacologic approaches diabetes treatment continuous review efficacy side effects burden
site:dha.gov.ae type 2 diabetes medication monitoring prerequisites telehealth
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic medicine start, stop, switch, titration or deintensification
medicine names, dose, route, frequency or targets
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Medication decisions require patient-specific clinician assessment.
No product or dosing content is mapped.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: People with diabetes receiving pharmacological treatment; diabetes type, age, pregnancy, comorbidity and therapy alter the review.
setting_applicability: Outpatient diabetes medication review; DHA evidence is adult type 2 diabetes telehealth.
UAE_applicability: DHA is UAE-direct for Dubai telehealth; UAE formulary, prescribing, insurance, renal, cardiovascular and endocrine pathways govern.
recency_verification: ADA Standards 2026 and DHA Issue 2 exact sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: Current registered official versions were identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact ADA person-centred treatment-review section supports interval change, functional impact, symptoms, relevant negatives, patient goals, clinician-reviewed laboratory and home-monitoring context, previous correspondence and questions; it does not select or change a medicine.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports documenting glycaemic and weight goals, comorbidities, adverse effects, hypoglycaemia, treatment burden, adherence or access, preferences and continuing clinician review without selecting a medicine.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact DHA monitoring-prerequisite section supports measured vital signs, weight or BMI, focused assessment and a clinician-entered plan or follow-up only after the clinician has reviewed monitoring and adherence context.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies clinician review of BP, creatinine, electrolytes, HbA1c, cholesterol, urine ACR, home glucose context, and adherence before medication decisions.

unresolved_source_gaps: Medication list, dose, adherence, access, adverse effects, hypoglycaemia, comorbidities, goals, laboratory values, treatment decision and follow-up interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
